qa_pairs.question: What are Meltdown and Spectre?
qa_pairs.answer: Meltdown and Spectre are bugs related to speculative execution in a variety of CPU architectures developed over the past ten to fifteen years, affecting processors from Intel and AMD, including those on Compute Canada clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What has Compute Canada done to address the Meltdown and Spectre vulnerabilities?
qa_pairs.answer: Compute Canada personnel have patched systems deemed sensitive to these vulnerabilities, and all vulnerable equipment operated by Compute Canada has been patched. New vendor patches will also be applied if and when they are released.

qa_pairs.question: What were the availability impacts of patching for Meltdown and Spectre?
qa_pairs.answer: Updates to patch the vulnerabilities required updating the operating system and rebooting the nodes. For compute nodes, this was typically done in a rolling fashion, was largely transparent to users, and is now complete.

qa_pairs.question: When were the Meltdown and Spectre updates applied at Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: Updates were applied at Graham between 2018 January 5 and January 31, with most nodes updated by January 13.

qa_pairs.question: What are the performance impacts of the operating system patches for Meltdown and Spectre?
qa_pairs.answer: Performance impacts vary; some cited figures are alarming (up to a 30% or even 50% performance hit), while others are very minimal.

qa_pairs.question: Which types of tasks are most heavily affected by the security patches?
qa_pairs.answer: Tasks that involve a lot of input/output (reading and writing files), such as databases or file transfers (e.g., rsync), seem to be most heavily affected.

qa_pairs.question: How are most high performance computing (HPC) jobs affected by the security patches?
qa_pairs.answer: Most high performance computing jobs should be minimally affected since the vast majority of their run time is spent computing rather than doing input and output.

qa_pairs.question: Do different processor generations experience different performance degradations due to the patches?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, different processor generations are affected to different degrees, with the most notable performance degradation reported for older processors.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I operate my own virtual machine on the Compute Canada Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are operating your own virtual machine in the Compute Canada cloud, you are responsible for updating its operating system frequently to include the latest security patches to address these bugs.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I believe my application is severely impacted by the security patches?
qa_pairs.answer: If you believe your application may be severely impacted by the security patches, please contact the Technical support team and bring forward comparative performance numbers of your application (e.g., job run times before and after the announcement).

qa_pairs.question: Is it always possible to mitigate the performance impact of the security patches?
qa_pairs.answer: Mitigating the performance impact of the security patches is likely to require some modification to the code you are running, and it may not always be possible.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find specific instructions on how to update Linux virtual machines for security patches?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find specific instructions on how to update Linux VMs by referring to the [[Security_considerations_when_running_a_VM#Updating_your_VM|updating your VM]] section.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more detailed information about the Meltdown and Spectre bugs?
qa_pairs.answer: A detailed discussion of the two bugs can be found on https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2018/01/meltdown-and-spectre-every-modern-processor-has-unfixable-security-flaws/.

qa_pairs.question: Should users be cautious when interpreting external performance comparison benchmarks related to Meltdown and Spectre?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users should keep in mind that external performance comparisons were not necessarily run on hardware and operating systems similar to what Compute Canada clusters are running.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find other general information about Spectre and Meltdown, including links to vendor patch sites?
qa_pairs.answer: Other general information about Spectre and Meltdown is available on the https://www.us-cert.gov/ncas/alerts/TA18-004A US-CERT web site, which includes comprehensive links to vendor patch sites.
